title: Moving a Private School Campus: Executing a Large-Scale Move During the Summer Break
excerpt: Private school campus move: summer break relocation, classrooms, labs, library, IT, phased logistics, large-scale commercial moving, Addis education sector.
content:
Moving a private school campus: executing a large-scale move during the summer break

Private school campus moves are city-within-city relocations—classrooms, science labs, libraries, admin, IT backbone, and sports storage must land before students return with backpacks and parents with expectations. Summer break compresses the window; rain and contractor overlap compress it further. Large-scale execution demands phased waves, labeled inventory by building zone, and RACI charts—not heroic weekend improvisation.

This guide covers campus moves during summer break in Addis Ababa.

Summer window planning (T-12 weeks)

  • Confirm handover dates for new campus buildings—unfinished wings kill schedules.
  • Inventory by zone: admin, primary, secondary, labs, library, sports.
  • IT and network vendor booked parallel to movers—not after truck.
  • Phased waves: labs and library early; admin and reception last for address continuity.

Labeling and asset tracking

Color by building wing; number sequentially—"SCI-LAB-042." Match fixed asset register for audit. IT assets serialized; chain-of-custody for servers.

Specialty rooms

Science labs

Chemical inventory per regulation; vendor pack for calibrated equipment. No casual box of glassware.

Library

Dewey-order carts or box numbering preserving shelf logic—re-shelving chaos costs September.

Sports and music

Heavy items rigged; pianos specialist if applicable.

Multiple trucks and stagger

Stagger arrivals—one convoy blocking campus gate angers neighbors and security. Parking plan per unloading zone at new campus.

Staff and volunteer coordination

One facilities director decides exceptions; teachers pack personal rooms to standard. Summer staff meals and shade for outdoor staging—heat exhaustion mislabels boxes.

Rain and Kremt contingency

Define reschedule triggers; cover loading bays; textbooks in sealed cartons off wet ground.

Reopening checklist

Fire exits clear; IT live; bathrooms functional; playground inspected—move complete only when registrar can enroll.

Active Movers institutional references

Request education-sector campus move history; disclose building count and lab presence at RFP.

Conclusion

Campus summer moves succeed with wave planning, asset labeling, IT parallelism, and reopen criteria—not when last truck leaves, but when first bell can ring.
author: Active Movers Team
